# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Node Dependencies Caching Workflow
on: [push] # Triggers when you push changes (i.e., code is pushed to your repository) or if there are new releases on GitHub Packages, pull requests etc...  You can change it according to the trigger source for this workflow in Github Actions
jobs:  
    build_and_cache: # Name of job that will run our steps. This name must be unique within a single repository (i.e., cannot use "build") or across all repositories, and can include alphanumeric characters only using underscores (_) if you want to have spaces in your cache keys
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Specify the type of machine that will run this job – i.e., Ubuntu latest is a recommended choice for CI/CD pipelines as it's more reliable and secure than any specific OS version (i.e, Windows or MacOS) in general cases
    steps:  
        - name: Checkout code # This action checks out your repository so you can use the actions去获取代码库，并将工作区设置为存储仓库的文件。 这使得您的工作目录与您的GitHub Actions运行环境保持一致（例如在CI/CD管道中运行的机器上执行命令`echo ${{ secrets.PAT }} | sudo -S git clone https://github.com/{username}/{repo}.git /tmp/{workspace} --depth 1; cd /tmp/{workspace}; pwd, ls
           with:   # Specify the type of source you want to checkout (e.g., branch name or commit hash) and path for storing repository files in your workspace area on runner machine。您可以使用`actions/checkout@v2 `来获取代码库，并将工作区设置为存储仓库的文件
             token: ${{ secrets.PAT }} # PAT is a Github Personal Access Token that you have created in your GitHub account to authenticate with the Actions service (i.e., run workflows) on behalf of users or applications, such as installing dependencies using `npm ci` command
             fetch-depth: 0   # Specify how deep we want git fetch/clone recursion set it by default is zero which means shallow clone only including immediate children and no submodules (i.e., everything in the repo) if you are going to use caching, otherwise full repository will be fetched
         - name: Cache node_modules # This action retrieves cached files from your workflow cache or runs specified steps on a new machine instance based of runner environment settings – i.e、 It tries to retrieve any previously saved data related with the "node modules" (i., `npm ci` command) and use it instead if available, otherwise run commands that install dependencies
           uses: actions/cache@v2 # Specify a particular step from another action's job using its id or name. In this case we are calling an existing 'actions/cache'. ‘uses’ can be used to import steps into workflow files and call them within the same file (i., `npm ci` command)
           with:   # Specify config for caching dependencies, you might want different cache directories depending on your project setup. You should also specify a key that will uniquely identify this particular job's runs of these actions in all possible runner environments to prevent duplications and ensure the correct behavior across jobs runners (i., `npm ci` command)
             path: ~/.npm # Points npm cache where it can store its data. This is set by default but you might want different paths depending on your project setup – i.e, It should be in a directory that'll not interfere with other installed packages or user-specific files (i., `rm -rf ~/.npm && mkdir .cache` command)
             key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }} # Specify the cache directory and a unique identifier for this job's runs, it will be used to restore cached dependencies on all possible runners (i., `npm ci` command)
         - name: Install Dependencies  # This action is going execute commands that install your project’s package-lock.json file using npm or yarn if the cache doesn contain a lockfile, otherwise it will use an empty directory to avoid reinstalling dependencies on every run (i., `npm ci` command)
           run: |   # Specify any steps you want in this action that should be executed within your job's workspace. These could include installing or updating packages and running tests if necessary – i.e, It can install all the needed dependencies with npm/yarn (i., `npm ci` command)
             ${{ env.YARN_TOKEN ? 'echo ${NPM_TOKEN} | sudo -S yarn add' : 'echo ${PAT} | sudo -S npm install --quiet' }} # Specify the token or PAT for authenticating with Actions service and installing dependencies (i., `npm ci` command)
